Journal.Title: Zhonghua liu xing bing xue za zhi = Zhonghua liuxingbingxue zazhi
Journal.ISOAbbreviation: Zhonghua Liu Xing Bing Xue Za Zhi

Article.ArticleTitle: [Teaching practice of medical statistics in the context of new medicine].

Abstract.AbstractText: The "Medicine+X" interdisciplinary integration talents cultivation is an important measure to implement the national strategy of "Education Power" and "Healthy China" and to promote the construction of new medicine. Taking the mandatory graduate course of "Fundamentals of Medical Statistics" at Peking University Health Science Center as an example, this paper systematically elaborates on the instructional design and implementation steps of the integrated medical statistics curriculum, and evaluates the effectiveness of the curriculum reform by analyzing the results of a questionnaire survey. The results indicated that after the teaching reform, the post-class test scores, learning benefit scores, and satisfaction scores of the students all significantly increased compared with the scores before the reform [(15.4±2.9) vs. (10.6±5.3), (91.2±9.1) vs. (80.3±29.0), and (99.5±0.6) vs. (97.3±1.9)], the differences were significant (all P<0.05). However, no significant differences were observed among the final exam scores (P=0.973). The teaching practice proved that, compared with traditional teaching modes, the integrated teaching curriculum showed superior effects on improving students' clinical parctice capability and strengthening the application of scientific research methods, providing robust support to the training of new-type medical talents who can have the performance inviolving the medical treatment, disease prevention and healthcare and further promote the in-depth development of medical science.

Grant.Agency: 2023 Medical Education Research Project of Medical Education Branch of Chinese Medical Association and National Medical Education Development Center

MedlineJournalInfo.MedlineTA: Zhonghua Liu Xing Bing Xue Za Zhi

OtherAbstract.AbstractText: 培养具备跨学科知识整合能力的“医学+X”复合型人才，是践行“教育强国”与“健康中国”国家战略、推动新医科领域发展的关键举措。本研究以北京大学医学部研究生必修课程《医学统计学基础》为例，系统阐述了医学统计学整合课程的教学设计与实施步骤，并通过问卷调查进行课程改革的效果评价。结果表明，课程改革后学生的课后测试成绩、学习收益评分与满意度较改革前均有明显升高［（15.4±2.9）分vs.（10.6±5.3）分，（91.2±9.1）分vs.（80.3±29.0）分，（99.5±0.6）分vs.（97.3±1.9）分］，差异有统计学意义（均P<0.05），而期末成绩差异无统计学意义（P=0.973）。实践证明，与传统教学模式相比，整合式课程在提升临床实践能力、强化科研方法学应用等方面展现出更优的教学效能，其教学改革范式可为构建集临床诊疗、疾病预防、健康管理于一体的复合型医学人才培养体系奠定重要的理论与实践基础，进一步推动新医科建设的深入发展。.
